nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Aye, we're here."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The captain calmly remembers the reason he aggred to follow you to the end of the world. "If it's not much to ask, pay up."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Whaaat ? After all this, not even a hug ?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Sure, when the job is done"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Sure, when the job is done (lie)"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "When the creatured is slain, we can talk"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "No", he frowns

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "You may be killed for all I know, but fine, I promised to take you here and I am a man of my word."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "So, Jade ... allow me to ask you a question, why come all this way," After months wondering, the captain decides it's time to ask the so important question.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Well, you know about them, I'm sure you heard the crew talking. My parents were killed by the creature 20 years ago ... It's my duty to avenge them"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Why I came to the end of the world to fight a creature that no one has killed before ? Money, what else could it be"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Truth is that I was bored, but I always liked the idea of becoming the greatest pirate the world has ever seen"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " You don't strike as the sentimental type, who could have thought your intentions were so noble. You know you will probably die right ?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " You can't be serious, " he screams laughing like a maniac. " Well, I knew I liked you for a reson. You know you will die right ? "

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " Before I let you go, " he stops, for a second there, you can even detect a sign of worry in his voice " just tell me a thing. are you even strong ? "

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: (You may select up to 15 points in Strength, Charisma and Intelligence in our SCI stats, for a max of 10 by stat. Be careful this is an important choice.)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I'm better with words than fighting." (2 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I may not be the best fighter in the world, But I have a chance. " (5 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I killed a lot of people and monsters in the path that lead me here, this time will not be different. " (8 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Well kid, I hope your boss pays me for my troubles after your death," he laughs for quite a while ... " At least you're more intelligent than you look right ?'

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Well kid, I respect you, I hope for the best," he said calmly, he wishes for the best, but he doubts being a good fighter will be enough " Are you as smart as you are good at using swords ?

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " Well, I'm not much of a reader, I prefer to learn by doing. " (2 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I know my way around a map and basic alchemy ... Well at least enough to survive. " (5 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I studied years for this back at the continent, my mind is my greatest weapon. " (8 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " All muscle, no brain " he sighs, rubbing his temples. " What about people? Can you at least talk your way out of a bad situation? "

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " A scholar, huh? Well, that big brain might just keep your head attached to your shoulders, " he nods approvingly. " But what about people? Can you talk your way out of a bad situation? "

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " Look, I was not the most popular girl in college " (2 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I can negotiate a fair price at the market, but I'm no diplomat. " (5 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " I could sell a drowning man a glass of water. " (8 as base stat)

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " Well, you better hope your sword or your spells do the talking then." He shakes his head, turning back to his ship. "Good luck Jade."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Ha! A silver tongue. Unfortunately for us, the monsters don't like to talk much '" He smirks, turning back to his ship. "Good luck Jade."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Thanks, I will need it"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Thanks, but no thanks, I don't believe in luck "

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Wait, could you tell me more about what happened here?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " What always happens, we had a good thing," he stops. He is serious for a change, It's clear he does not like to talk about it. " It was really something, but then someone or something came and destroyed it all. It's what always happens with us, people, we are not made to have a good and peaceful life. God always finds a way of fucking us in the ass when we least expect it."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " If by some miracle you aren't killed. Please kill them all. Every last of those animals." Even with all his calm demeanor, It's clear he is a victim of the creatures just like everyone else who ever lived on the Verge. The captain turns his back to you and waits for you to go somewhere else. '
